key_departures.impact: Robinson was the Giants' lead receiver in 2025 after Nabers went down to ACL tear, finishing with 92 catches for 1,014 yards and 4 TDs. His departure leaves a significant slot/production void. Giants did not retain him despite interest.

Flott signed a 3yr/$45M deal with $32M fully guaranteed with Tennessee. He started 24 games in his final two seasons with NYG, had 11 passes defended in 2025. A real loss from the secondary, which was already a need.
Released by Giants for $9M cap savings. Had 143 tackles in 2025 (4th most in franchise history). A veteran captain cut to open cap space and allow Edmunds signing.
Bellinger's role had diminished with Theo Johnson's arrival. Just 19 receptions in 2025. Departure was expected with Likely incoming. Minimal impact.

Lost valuable center/guard swing depth to Titans on a 2yr/$9M deal. Schlottmann was the Giants' only reliable backup center; his departure leaves John Michael Schmitz (injury-prone) with no experienced backup at C.
Special teams leader who never locked down a starting role. Depth/ST loss; Giants still have Tyler Nubin and Jevon Holland.
Backup QB behind Jaxson Dart. Not expected to return. Low impact on team needs given Dart's status as starter.
Six-year Giants long snapper and former captain. Departure is a special teams note but not a positional need for the draft.

signings.analysis: Giants retain their starting RT on a 3yr/$39M deal with $25.45M guaranteed (per Spotrac/Big Blue View). Eluemunor had a career-best season in 2025, playing 1,088 snaps with a 88.8% pass block win rate. Paired with Andrew Thomas on the left, the Giants have a secure starting tackle tandem through at least 2027. RT goes from a significant need to a locked position. Age (31 turning 32) and the 3-year term signal a bridge rather than a dynasty-level solution, but the 2-year effective guarantee window is real. OT is no longer a draft priority.

3yr/$40M deal (upside to $47.5M per Jordan Schultz) makes Likely the 3rd-highest paid TE in the NFL at signing. Harbaugh reunion piece — Likely played all 4 seasons under him in Baltimore. With Theo Johnson already on roster, Giants now have 12-personnel as their base identity. Likely is a primary receiving TE who will be Dart's most trusted target. $0 in fully guaranteed money per OTC (injury offset and escalator structure), but the AAV and term signal a real starter commitment. TE goes from a notable need to a strength. Draft TE is no longer necessary early.

3yr/$36M with $23.7M guaranteed (per OTC/Spotrac/Rapoport). Direct replacement for released Bobby Okereke. Edmunds, 27, was a 2x Pro Bowler with the Bills and started all 119 career games he played. Has 100+ tackles in every season played. However, he missed the final 4 games of 2025 with a groin injury and was cut by Chicago entering the final year of his $72M deal. Giants still may want a Day 2/3 LB prospect given the draft class depth. Partially addresses LB — starter secured, but Micah McFadden's 1yr re-sign (depth) and the youth question means LB remains a draft consideration.

1yr/$10M (per Spotrac). Former first-round pick (26th overall, 2021) on a prove-it deal. Has 71 career games with 58 starts, 43 passes defensed. Injuries have derailed a promising career — missed significant time with Cleveland and was traded to Jacksonville mid-2025 season. One-year structure is the tell: Giants want upside but aren't committing. Loses Flott (3yr/$45M) and gains Newsome (1yr/$10M) — net downgrade in commitment. CB remains a pressing need heading into the draft, especially with Paulson Adebo as the only locked-in starter. CB moves from tier 1 to tier 2 — partially addressed but not solved.

1yr/$4.5M (per ESPN/Adam Schefter). Slot receiver option to partially replace Wan'Dale Robinson's production. Austin brings elite speed and return value. 84 career catches for 1,100 yards in 48 games. One-year depth deal — not a starter-caliber signing at the market-rate level. WR remains a meaningful draft need with Nabers' ACL recovery timeline uncertain.

1yr/$10M up to $10M (per ESPN/Rapoport). Released as Falcons cap casualty. Had 64 catches for 992 yards and career-high 5 TDs in 2024 before a down 2025 (32/443/1 TD, collarbone injury). Mid-tier prove-it deal at a position where NYG lost their leading receiver. Nabers' return (ACL) is the key variable. With Mooney + Austin + Hodgins + Slayton, WR depth is adequate but uninspiring. 1-year deal means WR is still a legitimate mid-round draft target. Partially addresses the post-Robinson hole; need drops from tier 1 critical to tier 2.

2yr/$7.63M with $3.54M guaranteed (per Big Blue View breakdown). 6x Pro Bowl fullback, 3x All-Pro including 1st-team All-Pro in 2025. Scheme-defining signing for Harbaugh's run-heavy identity — reunites with the FB he coached for 9 years in Baltimore. Ricard is the highest-paid fullback in the league. FB was not a pre-FA need but this creates an entirely new schematic dimension. No draft impact at traditional positions.

1yr/$3M (per SNY/Connor Hughes). Third safety behind Tyler Nubin and Jevon Holland. Harbaugh connection from Baltimore. Coming off torn Achilles (missed most of 2025). Played all 17 games in 2024 with 10 starts for Ravens. This is a low-cost, high-ceiling bet on health. Depth move only. S was not a critical pre-FA need and remains a minor consideration.
1yr/$1.4875M (per Big Blue View). Former Giants starting safety returns from SF where he started 7 games in 2025. Veteran minimum-tier re-sign for secondary depth and ST. No meaningful need impact.
Minimum deal. Special teams specialist from Miami. 24 career tackles, 21 on ST. Camp body/ST depth only. No positional need impact.

3yr/$12.3M with $7.3M guaranteed (per Spotrac/OTC). NFL's highest-paid punter. 2025 first-team All-Pro, led league in net punting average (44.9). Replaces Jamie Gillan (released). Pure special teams upgrade — not a positional draft need.
1yr deal (terms TBD beyond estimated $2M). Former 1st-team All-Pro kicker. Missed 2025 season with hip injury. Will compete with Ben Sauls. One-year deal reflects injury uncertainty. Pure ST move.

1yr/$3.75M with up to $2M in incentives (per ESPN). Re-signed as depth LB alongside Edmunds. Had 100+ tackles in 2023 and 2024 before injury in 2025. Provides rotational depth but is not a starter-level solution. Paired with Edmunds, this brings LB from a top need to a moderate one.
Low-cost 1yr re-sign. Physical WR who fits Harbaugh's blocking scheme on the outside. Depth piece only. WR need still exists.

1yr/veteran minimum (per Big Blue View — Giants recoup full $1.075M if he doesn't make roster). 2022 7th-overall pick who never lived up to his draft position. Harbaugh sees reclamation potential. Can provide emergency tackle depth or attempt a position switch inside. Minimal impact on OT need (Eluemunor handles that); could help IOL depth.
Low-cost 1yr re-sign for interior line depth. Giants still need guard help beyond Stinnie. Depth-only move with John Michael Schmitz as starting C and Schlottmann gone. IOL remains a real draft need.
1yr minimum re-sign. ST/slot depth only. No meaningful impact on WR need.
RFA tender re-sign. Primarily a ST contributor. No meaningful impact on LB starter need.
ERFA tender. Practice squad project elevated for another year. No impact on starting EDGE depth.
ERFA tender. Physical corner and best punt gunner on team. ST specialist only — CB starter need unchanged.
Veteran minimum 1yr re-sign. Blocking TE depth behind Likely and Theo Johnson. No need impact — TE is now a strength.
Restructured contract to stay with Giants at reduced cap number. Depth RB behind Cam Skattebo and Tyrone Tracy Jr. No positional need impact.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Eluemunor re-sign (3yr/$39M, $25.45M gtd) locks in the starting RT. Andrew Thomas holds LT. Bookend tackles are now secured through 2027 minimum. OT is no longer a draft priority — only developmental depth at best.

Isaiah Likely (3yr/$40M, elite AAV) is a bonafide starter and Harbaugh's offensive centerpiece. Paired with Theo Johnson, Giants are TE-rich. This position is a strength now — remove from needs board entirely.

Okereke release created the need; Edmunds signing (3yr/$36M, $23.7M gtd) partially fills it. Edmunds is a clear starter with 119-game starting track record, but his groin injury and Bears release create health/durability questions. McFadden back as rotational depth. Giants would still benefit from a Day 2 LB prospect (deep class this year) to build for the future — need drops from tier 1 critical to tier 3 value.

Lost Flott (3yr/$45M to Titans) — a real starter — and replaced with Newsome on a 1yr/$10M prove-it deal. Newsome has starting pedigree but injury history is concerning. Adebo is the only locked-in CB1 on the roster. CB remains a genuine priority in the draft — likely Rounds 1 or 2. Drops from tier 1 (acute) to tier 2 (still important) because Newsome adds legitimate competition.

Lost Robinson (92/1014/4 in 2025) to Titans. Added Mooney (1yr/$10M) and Calvin Austin (1yr/$4.5M). Both are one-year depth signings. Nabers' ACL recovery timeline is uncertain. Mooney had a significant down year in 2025. The depth is fine but the starting quality post-Nabers-absence is fragile. WR stays at tier 2 — remains a real Round 2-3 target especially if Nabers' return is at risk.

Schlottmann's departure to Titans removes the only experienced C backup behind injury-prone John Michael Schmitz. Guard depth is also thin with Stinnie (minimum) and Neal (minimum reclamation) as the backups. No starter-level IOL was signed. Need stays high — interior OL (particularly G and C depth) is a real Round 2-4 draft target. Drops from tier 1 only because the starting group (Schmitz at C, Jon Runyan at G) is still intact.

No meaningful EDGE signings or departures. Brian Burns ($36.55M cap hit) and Kayvon Thibodeaux are the starters. EDGE need is unchanged from pre-FA — still a position to address in the draft if value presents, particularly given Thibodeaux's contract year situation.
Dexter Lawrence anchors the interior. No significant IDL moves in FA. Need unchanged — Giants could still use an interior pass rusher or rotational piece in the mid-rounds.

key_additions.headline: Harbaugh reunion piece and Dart's new security blanket; 3rd-highest paid TE makes NYG 12-personnel a base identity
Two-time Pro Bowler replaces Okereke as defensive QB, bringing 900 career tackles and 14 interceptions to a much-needed LB overhaul
Giants lock in starting RT to protect Dart; bookend tackles secured with Andrew Thomas through 2027
Former first-round pick on prove-it deal attempts to stabilize a CB room gutted by Flott's departure
Speed and outside threat joins Nabers and Slayton to replace Robinson's production — 992 yards and 5 TDs in 2024 peak
Six-time Pro Bowl fullback follows Harbaugh to New York, becoming the NFL's highest-paid FB to lead-block for Cam Skattebo
2025 first-team All-Pro punter (50.1-yd avg, NFL-leading 44.9 net) becomes league's highest-paid punter in Harbaugh ST overhaul

key_losses.headline: Giants' 2025 leading receiver (92/1014/4 TDs) departs as a free agent, leaving a significant slot production hole
Developing starter (24 starts in final 2 seasons, 11 PDs in 2025) commands top-market CB deal NYG couldn't match
Three-year Giants captain and 143-tackle performer cut for cap room to facilitate Edmunds signing
Underrated swing C/G departs, leaving Giants with no experienced backup behind injury-prone John Michael Schmitz
